mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 3.1% signals inflationary pressures that prompt central banks to tighten policy, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite inflationary signal, or CPIAUCSL fails to correlate with rate hikes in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust demand conditions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in co-expansionary periods fall below 0.5% annually, or GDPC1/HOUST correlation with SPX drops below 0.2 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5pp monthly decline in unemployment (UNRATE) combined with rising payrolls (PAYEMS) signals tightening labor markets, creating inflationary wage pressures that drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls > 0.5pp and PAYEMS rises > 100k in 1 month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or wage growth fails to correlate with equity underperformance in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening supply-side capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through reduced corporate profitability expectations.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in INDPRO decline periods exceed 1.2% annually, or INDPRO fails to predict equity returns in 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 2% quarterly decline in real personal income (RSAFS) indicates deteriorating consumer demand, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through reduced corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in RSAFS decline periods exceed 0.8% annually, or RSAFS fails to correlate with equity underperformance in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3pp monthly increase in unemployment (UNRATE) during rising wage growth (PAYEMS) signals labor market overheating, prompting central bank intervention that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp and PAYEMS rises > 150k in 1 month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or labor market signals fail to predict equity returns in 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 10 basis point monthly increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) during rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals aggressive monetary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 10 bps and CPIAUCSL rises > 0.4% in 1 month
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in tightening cycles exceed 1.0% annually, or rate/inflation signals fail to predict equity underperformance in 3-year rolling windows.
